title: Mud, Cold and Neat Rocks
summary:
After leaving St. George, we headed up towards the area surrounding Bryce Canyon National Park. We failed to check the weather forecast before heading that direction and were surprised to find a large quantity of snow as we headed over a mountain pass on the drive. Luckily, we dropped back down in elevation and most of the snow was gone by the time we found a campsite near Losee Canyon outside the park. However, the overnight temperatures were in the low 30s, which made for some cold nights, especially considering that our van's heater decided to stop working. The days were nice and sunny so we decided to go for a bike ride up Losee Canyon. Unfortunately, the recent snow made the trails extremely muddy. So much so that our wheels ceased to spin from the buildup of mud on the tires. The rest of our visit was spent cooking some tasty dinners, watching the new Game of Thrones spin-off series, and meticulously washing the mud off of our bikes. Right before taking off, we went on a neat hike right from camp that meandered through some sandstone hoodoos and natural arches.

-Colin

photos.caption: On the drive, we drove over a mountain pass and were surprised to find snow. We took a brief pit stop so Maya could have some snowy play time.
There was a trail right near our campsite that meandered through some sandstone hoodoos and natural arches.
The bike-halting mud that we encountered on our ride.
Maya and Helen hunkering down in the cold temps.
